```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  p <- sample(setdiff(seq(0.05,0.95,0.05),0.5),1)
  vals <- round(c(p,1-p,p^2,p*(1-p),1),3)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.3f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Se $I_A=\mathbf{1}_A$ e $P(A)=`r p`$, qual é $E(I_A)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
A indicadora vale 1 em $A$ e 0 fora de $A$:
$$E(I_A)=1P(A)+0P(A^c)=P(A)=`r p`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S8Q6_esperanca_indicadora
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
